title: Multi-platform censorship-resistant publishing
description: Publish oilcloth's intelligence site to censorship-resistant platforms: IPFS, Tor (.onion), i2p (.i2p), and upcoming Iceland VPS for clearnet. Same MkDocs build output across all platforms. Research hosting requirements and automation for each.

title: Integrate fabric-ai into oilcloth
description:
Enable oilcloth to use fabric patterns, especially YouTube extraction.

## Phase 1: Container Integration (current)
- Add fabric binary to container image
- Install yt-dlp for YouTube transcript extraction
- Mount ~/.config/fabric for patterns and API config
- Create MCP tool for fabric calls

## Phase 2: REST API (future)
- Run fabric --serve as systemd service
- Expose to multiple agents (not just oilcloth)
- Investigate fabric-mcp for cleaner integration

## Key Use Cases
- YouTube video summarization (extract_wisdom, youtube_summary)
- Content analysis patterns (summarize, extract_ideas, etc.)
- 237 patterns available

## References
- https://github.com/danielmiessler/fabric
- https://github.com/ksylvan/fabric-mcp
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: fabric integrated into container with MCP tools

title: MkDocs maintenance agent/skill
description: Build a Claude skill or agent that can maintain oilcloth's MkDocs intelligence site. Research existing MkDocs skills/automation. Agent handles site rebuilds, navigation updates, new page scaffolding, theme customization. Currently serving at ph0x.ratfish-coho.ts.net via tailscale.

title: Research MkDocs Claude skills and automation
description: Find anyone who has built Claude Code skills, MCP tools, or automation for MkDocs. Check GitHub, Claude community, MCP registries. Need patterns to steal - theme management, content pipelines, build automation, nav generation. Informs the MkDocs agent design.
notes: Research complete. No existing Claude Code MkDocs skill. Three MCP servers exist (douinc/mkdocs-mcp-plugin is best). Material Insiders now free but sunsetting for Zensical (Nov 2026). Best pattern: awesome-pages-plugin with .pages files per directory. Red Hat TechDocs AI agent is closest prior art. Full report in session transcript.

title: Agent team architecture - oilcloth PR system
description: Design and build an agent team supporting oilcloth's mission. Roles: content manager (editorial, scheduling, consistency), creative director (design, presentation, impact), PR/distribution (publishing pipeline, audience). Oilcloth is evolving from researcher to publisher. She is our Paladin.

title: YouTube Shorts automated publishing pipeline
description: Automated YouTube Shorts pipeline for investigative journalism. TAL/Ezra Klein storytelling style. Stack: Remotion (video), Kokoro TTS (voice on 5070 GPU), googleapis (upload). Dossiers → narrative script → voice → video → YouTube.

title: YouTube API compliance audit + OAuth verification
description: Submit YouTube API compliance audit (unlocks public uploads) and OAuth consent screen verification (persistent refresh tokens). Audit form: https://support.google.com/youtube/contact/yt_api_form. Need: privacy policy page, demo video, scope justifications. GCP project: oilcloth-osint-purple. Timeline: 1-4 weeks.

title: Get OAuth refresh token for YouTube API
description: Run one-time interactive OAuth flow to get refresh token. Use client_secret JSON in project root. Store refresh token in .env. Move OAuth consent screen to Production mode so tokens don't expire every 7 days.

title: TTS integration — Kokoro (local) + ElevenLabs (production)
description: Install Docker Desktop + WSL2 on Windows box. Run Kokoro-FastAPI GPU container (ghcr.io/remsky/kokoro-fastapi-gpu:latest) on port 8880. Expose over Tailscale. OpenAI-compatible API. Best voices: af_heart, af_bella.

title: Set up Remotion project for video templates
description: npx create-video@latest in video-pipeline/. Build 9:16 vertical templates: document reveal, timeline, data visualization, kinetic typography. TAL/Ezra Klein visual style - clean, source-attributed, emotionally resonant.

title: Build story agent prompt (TAL/Ezra Klein narrator)
description: Claude prompt that transforms an OSINT dossier into a TAL/Ezra Klein-style narrative script. Structure: hook → person → facts → systemic meaning. Outputs structured JSON with scenes, narration text, visual cues, and timing. This is the creative core.

title: Build end-to-end pipeline: dossier → video → YouTube
description: Node.js orchestrator: read dossier from groups/main/osint/, run story agent, call Kokoro TTS for narration, render with Remotion, upload via googleapis. Schedule/cron support.

title: Write privacy policy for YouTube API audit
description: Simple privacy policy page for the oilcloth YouTube channel. Must mention Google/YouTube data handling. Host somewhere accessible (GitHub Pages, site, etc). Required for both compliance audit and OAuth verification.

title: Copyright and licensing strategy for YouTube channel
description: Need DMCA takedown power to protect narrative integrity while keeping content non-commercial. Evaluate CC BY-NC-ND vs full copyright retention. Note: CC0 (used on privacy policy) explicitly waives DMCA rights — content licensing must be different from site licensing. Long game, not urgent.

title: Multi-language video production
description: Produce YouTube Shorts in multiple languages natively. Kokoro already supports: Spanish (ef_dora), French (ff_siwis), Hindi (hf_alpha/hf_beta, hm_omega/hm_psi), Italian (if_sara, im_nicola), Japanese (jf_*, jm_*), Portuguese (pf_dora, pm_*), Chinese (zf_*, zm_*), British English (bf_*, bm_*). Pipeline: Claude translates script, Kokoro generates native-language audio, visual text swapped to target language. Spanish is minimum viable.

title: Filesystem-based skills pattern for oilcloth
description:
Implement Daniel Miessler's filesystem-based skills pattern as an alternative/complement to MCP tools.

## Background

Source: https://danielmiessler.com/blog/anthropic-downplays-mcps

Instead of MCP tool definitions (150k tokens), expose skills as TypeScript/bash files that oilcloth discovers and executes on-demand (2k tokens). 98.7% token savings.

## Knowledge Base

See: `groups/main/knowledge/filesystem-skills.md`

## Architecture

```
/workspace/group/skills/
├── README.md              # What skills are available
├── messaging/
│   ├── send_discord.ts    # Send message to Discord
│   └── README.md
├── scheduling/
│   ├── create_task.ts     # Schedule a task
│   ├── list_tasks.ts
│   └── README.md
├── fabric/
│   ├── analyze_claims.ts  # Wrapper for fabric pattern
│   ├── youtube_wisdom.ts  # YouTube extraction
│   └── README.md
├── osint/
│   ├── create_dossier.sh  # Already exists!
│   ├── analyze_dossier.sh
│   └── README.md
└── gmail/
    ├── search_emails.ts
    ├── send_email.ts
    └── README.md
```

## Phases

### Phase 1: Foundation
- Create skills/ directory structure in container
- Create README.md convention for discoverability
- Migrate existing OSINT scripts to skills/osint/
- Update oilcloth CLAUDE.md to explain skills pattern

### Phase 2: Core Skills
- Wrap existing MCP tools as skills (messaging, scheduling)
- Create fabric skills (analyze_claims, youtube_wisdom)
- Test token usage reduction

### Phase 3: Gmail Skills
- Wrap gmail MCP as filesystem skills
- Compare token usage vs MCP approach

### Phase 4: Evaluation
- Measure actual token savings
- Assess agent effectiveness with skills vs MCP
- Document learnings

## Success Criteria

- oilcloth can discover and use skills via filesystem
- Token usage measurably reduced
- Skills are self-documenting (README.md pattern)
- MCP remains available as fallback

## Open Questions

- Keep MCP tools alongside skills, or migrate entirely?
- How to handle skills that need host-side execution (IPC)?
- Should skills be executable or just documentation for agent to implement?

title: Realtime NanoClaw Dashboard
description: Simple dashboard to see what oilcloth is doing in real-time. Show incoming messages, agent activity, responses sent.

title: Terminal dashboard UI
description: Web-based dashboard UI. Simple HTML/CSS/JS page showing: incoming messages, current agent status, outgoing responses. Served from nanoclaw itself or separate lightweight server.

title: Log streaming from systemd
description: Capture and parse journalctl output for nanoclaw service. Extract message events, container starts, responses.

title: Container activity monitor
description: Show when Docker containers spin up/down for agent runs. Display duration and status.

title: Iceland VPS provisioning
description: Provision Iceland-based VPS for clearnet hosting of intelligence site. Iceland chosen for press freedom protections and distance from US jurisdiction. Serves same MkDocs static output as other platforms.

title: OSINT Capability for oilcloth
description:
Build OSINT intelligence gathering capabilities for oilcloth.

## Goals
- Username enumeration across platforms
- Content aggregation (Lemmy, Digg)
- Phone/email lookup
- Fabric integration for content processing

## Approach
- Knowledge-based methodology (teach oilcloth techniques)
- Script library for reliable lookups
- Fabric patterns for processing

## Resources
- Rae Baker's OSINT tools guide
- Fabric framework
- Bellingcat/Bazzell methodology

See docs/OSINT-PLANNING.md for full planning document.

title: Username enumeration system
description:
Build username search capability using WhatsMyName.app and similar tools.

- API integration or scraping approach
- Cross-reference results
- Handle false positives
- Output structured report

title: Lemmy/Digg content aggregation
description:
Build content search across federated platforms.

- Query multiple Lemmy instances
- Check new Digg API/RSS
- Aggregate and dedupe results
- Format for easy consumption

title: Phone/email lookup tools
description:
Integrate public record lookup capabilities.

- Epieos for email → account discovery
- Phone carrier lookup (NumVerify or similar)
- HaveIBeenPwned integration
- Holehe for email site checks

title: Fabric integration
description:
Install Fabric and create OSINT-specific patterns.

- Install Fabric in container or as external tool
- Create patterns: extract_entities, verify_claim, summarize_profile
- Test with real content

title: OSINT knowledge base for oilcloth
description:
Create methodology documentation that oilcloth can reference.

- Verification techniques (Bellingcat style)
- Tool usage guides
- Best practices
- Legal/ethical boundaries
